clinical_rationale: 67F POD2 post robotic partial nephrectomy presenting with fever (38.9°C), sustained tachycardia (HR 112), rising WBC (18.2 × 10⁹/L), and progressive wound erythema. Clinical trajectory is deteriorating. NEWS2 score 7 — emergency escalation threshold. Pattern consistent with early post-operative infection / haematoma formation. Recommend urgent senior surgical review and cross-sectional imaging.

news2.news2_clinical_response: Emergency response — continuous monitoring, urgent senior clinical review

sepsis_screen.sepsis_action: Activate sepsis protocol. Blood cultures, lactate, broad-spectrum antibiotics within 1 hour. Assess for source control.

sbar.situation: URGENT: 67F POD2 post robotic partial nephrectomy — clinical deterioration with fever, tachycardia, rising WBC. NEWS2 = 7. Copilot recommends immediate escalation.
sbar.background: 67-year-old female underwent robotic-assisted partial right nephrectomy for a 3.2 cm renal mass. PMH: HTN, Type 2 DM. Discharged to ward POD1. Now POD2 with worsening clinical picture over past 6 hours.
sbar.assessment: Temp 38.9°C, HR 112 bpm, BP 98/64 mmHg, RR 24/min, SpO2 96% on 2L NC. WBC 18.2, CRP 142. Wound site shows erythema and induration. Lactate 2.4 mmol/L. qSOFA 2 — high sepsis likelihood. CT abdomen/pelvis being arranged.
sbar.recommendation: Review patient at bedside urgently. Consider CT imaging, blood cultures × 2, urine C&S, IV antibiotics per local protocol. Escalate to ICU if haemodynamics do not improve within 1 hour. Patient has been made NBM in anticipation of possible return to theatre.
